title: Tirumala Venkateswara Temple, Tirupati
intro: The Sri Tirumala Venkateswara Temple, also known as the Tirupati Balaji Temple, is one of the most famous and prosperous temples in India.
description:
Sri Tirumala Venkateswara Temple – Tirupati Balaji
Introduction
The Sri Tirumala Venkateswara Temple, also known as the Tirupati Balaji Temple, is one of the most famous and prosperous temples in India. It is dedicated to Lord Vishnu and is located on the Tirumala Hills in Andhra Pradesh.
The temple is dedicated to Lord Sri Venkateswara (Balaji), an avatar of Lord Vishnu. Every year, millions of devotees visit this place to have darshan and receive the blessings of Lord Balaji.
Temple Information
 Temple Name: Sri Tirumala Venkateswara Temple
 Other Names: Tirupati Balaji Temple, Sri Venkateswara Swamy Temple
 Location: Tirumala, Tirupati District, Andhra Pradesh 517504, India
 Presiding Deity: Lord Sri Venkateswara (an avatar of Lord Vishnu)
 Nearest Major City: Tirupati (approx. 22 km)
History and Significance
The Tirumala Venkateswara Temple is one of the holiest Vaishnavite pilgrimage sites in Hinduism. This temple is a primary center for the worship of Lord Vishnu in his Sri Venkateswara form.
It is believed that Lord Venkateswara resides on the Tirumala Hills in the Kali Yuga for the protection and welfare of his devotees. The temple is world-famous for its vast devotional tradition, daily worship rituals, service traditions, and spiritual atmosphere.
How to Reach
✈️ By Air
 Tirupati International Airport (TIR): Distance from the temple is approx. 40 km. Taxi and APSRTC bus services are available from the airport to Tirumala.
🚆 By Train
 Tirupati Railway Station: Distance from the temple is approx. 22 km. Regular bus services are available from Tirupati to Tirumala.
🛣️ By Road
Tirumala is well-connected by road to major cities.
Distances:
 Chennai – approx. 135 km
 Bengaluru – approx. 250 km
 Hyderabad – approx. 560 km
 Vijayawada – approx. 400 km
You can reach here by bus, taxi, or private vehicle.
Best Time to Visit
The Tirumala Temple remains open throughout the year.
For travel: September to March is considered more convenient according to the weather.
Special Occasions:
 Brahmotsavam
 Vaikunta Ekadashi
 Ratha Saptami
 Vasantotsav
Temple Timings
General Timings:
🕒 Approx. 2:30 AM to 11:30 PM
(Darshan timings may change based on ticket types, special services, and festivals.)
Special Sevas
Prominent services for Lord Venkateswara at the Tirumala Temple:
 Suprabhatam
 Thomala Seva
 Archana
 Kalyanotsavam
 Sahasra Deepalankarana Seva
Nearby Attractions
 Sri Padmavathi Ammavari Temple: Famous temple dedicated to Goddess Padmavathi, the consort of Lord Venkateswara.
 Kapila Theertham: A sacred place with natural waterfalls and religious significance.
 ISKCON Tirupati: A beautiful temple dedicated to Lord Krishna.
 Sri Venkateswara Zoological Park: One of the leading zoological parks in India.
 Akasa Ganga: Famous holy water source in Tirumala.
Dress Code
Wearing traditional Indian clothing is considered appropriate in the temple.
Men:
 Dhoti and Kurta
 Shirt and traditional pants
Women:
 Saree
 Salwar Kameez
 Other modest traditional clothing
Facilities for Devotees
 Darshan arrangements
 Online service and darshan facilities
 Prasadam distribution
 Accommodation arrangements for devotees
 Food facilities
 Help centers

faq.q1: Which deity is the Tirupati Balaji Temple dedicated to?
faq.a1: This temple is dedicated to Lord Shri Venkateswara, an incarnation of Lord Vishnu.
faq.q2: Where is the Tirupati Balaji Temple located?
faq.a2: It is located on the Tirumala Hills, Tirupati District, Andhra Pradesh.
faq.q3: Which is the nearest railway station to the Tirumala temple?
faq.q4: What is the best time to visit the Tirupati Balaji Temple?
faq.a4: The weather is most pleasant from September to March.
faq.q5: What are the main services (Sevas) performed at the Tirumala Temple?
faq.a5: Suprabhata Seva, Archana, Kalyanotsavam, and Sahasra Deepalankara Seva are among the prominent ones.
